properties.remark: OFF DUTY STATE TROOPER REPORTED WATER RISING RAPIDLY ALONG THE LITTLE MISSOURI RIVER AND STARTING TO OVERFLOW ITS BANKS. EVACUATIONS OF SEVERAL HOMES WERE ALREADY OCCURRING IN THE AREA.
LOCAL COORDINATOR CONFIRMED WATER SPOUT ON BULL SHOALS LAKE.

SHED BLOWN OVER IN ALPENA.
HAIL THE SIZE OF QUARTERS WAS COVERING THE GROUND.
SOME WINDOWS AND PORTION OF END WALL BLOWN OUT OF TOP FLOOR OF TWO STORY BUILDING. OCCURRED ON 400 BLOCK OF NORTH ARKANSAS AVENUE.
NEAR EXIT 123 OF INTERSTATE 30.
HAIL COVERING ROADWAY.

HAIL FELL NEAR THE BACK GATE OF THE BASE ALONG HWY 107.

TORNADO BEGAN SW OF BURLINGTON AND TRACKED EAST SOUTHEAST FOR 6.9 MILES. TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. DAMAGE WAS TO A TURKEY HOUSE WEST OF U.S. HIGHWAY 65...ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. HOUSES WERE DAMAGED EAST OF OLD HIGHWAY 65...AND SEVERAL BUILDINGS AT THE ARKANSAS BAPTIST BOYS RANCH WERE DAMAGED. THE TORNADO THEN DAMAGED A CHICKEN HOUSE OFF OF COTTONWOOD ROAD BEFORE DISSIPATING.
SOME TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
A HOUSE HAD DAMAGE NORTH OF HARRISON NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CENTER LOOP AND QUINCY LANE. CORRECTED TIME.
A TRAILER WAS KNOCKED OFF ITS FOUNDATION NEAR CENTER LOOP AND DUBUQUE ROAD. ALSO...WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT OF A HOUSE.

DAMAGE MAINLY CONSISTED OF SNAPPED OR UPROOTED TREES. THERE WAS ALSO MINOR DAMAGE TO OUTBUILDINGS AND CHICKEN HOUSES.
